dimensions.what_it_captures: Whether the individual processes ads through emotional triggers or logical evaluation of claims.
dimensions.concept_test_impact: Emotional responders favor concepts highlighting feel-good benefits; rational responders prefer detailed ingredient and efficacy data.
dimensions.ad_test_impact: Emotional ads with mood-driven narratives yield higher engagement for emotional processors; fact-based ads perform better for rational processors.
dimensions.what_we_can_infer: From Q6 'emotional' and Q13 ingredient-focused, mixed signals; thus, position on this dimension remains uncertain.
dimensions.example_variation: Twin A (emotional) clicks an ad showing joyful bathing; Twin B (rational) engages with an ad listing clinical trial results.

dimensions.dimension_name: Visual vs Textual Content Consumption Preference
dimensions.what_it_captures: Preference for absorbing information through visual media versus written text in product communications.
dimensions.concept_test_impact: Visual-preferring twins respond better to concept images and videos; text-preferring twins engage more with written descriptions and specs.
dimensions.ad_test_impact: Video and image-heavy ads capture visual twins' attention; carousel ads with text details appeal to textual twins.
dimensions.what_we_can_infer: Q59 mentions celebrity and expert review content, but format unspecified; no clear indication of preference.
dimensions.example_variation: For an Instagram ad, Twin A watches the full video; Twin B scrolls past but reads the caption thoroughly.

dimensions.dimension_name: Aspirational Lifestyle vs Relatable Everyday Appeal
dimensions.what_it_captures: Degree to which the individual identifies with and is motivated by aspirational messaging versus relatable, practical scenarios.
dimensions.concept_test_impact: Aspirational twins are drawn to premium, luxury concepts; relatable twins prefer value-oriented, daily-use positioning.
dimensions.ad_test_impact: Ads depicting idealised self-care routines resonate with aspirational twins; ads showing common grooming challenges appeal to relatable twins.
dimensions.what_we_can_infer: Q6 'exploratory' might indicate aspirational leanings, but Q10's practical routine suggests relatability; uncertainty remains.
dimensions.example_variation: An ad featuring a spa-like experience: Twin A feels inspired and considers purchase; Twin B finds it unrealistic and disengages.

dimensions.dimension_name: Impulse Purchase Tendency vs Deliberative Decision-Making
dimensions.what_it_captures: Propensity to make spontaneous purchase decisions when encountering product concepts or ads versus requiring careful consideration.
dimensions.concept_test_impact: Impulsive twins show higher immediate purchase intent; deliberative twins need more time and information before deciding.
dimensions.ad_test_impact: Time-sensitive offers and 'buy now' prompts trigger impulsive twins; deliberative twins respond better to detailed comparisons and reviews.
dimensions.what_we_can_infer: Q6 'impulsive' suggests high impulse, but brand loyalty (Q17) indicates consistency, making ad-context behavior uncertain.
dimensions.example_variation: A limited-time discount ad: Twin A clicks and buys immediately; Twin B bookmarks for later research.

dimensions.what_it_captures: How external factors like mood, environment, and social context influence the individual's engagement with ads and product concepts.
dimensions.concept_test_impact: Concepts presented during positive moods or relaxed states receive higher ratings; stressful contexts lower receptivity.
dimensions.ad_test_impact: Ads seen in leisure settings (e.g., at home) garner more attention than those in busy contexts (e.g., commuting).
dimensions.what_we_can_infer: No questions address context effects; water quality influence (Q34) on products hints at environmental sensitivity, but not for ads.
dimensions.example_variation: The same ad viewed while stressed: Twin A ignores it; when viewed while relaxed, Twin A engages and considers purchase.
